sections.paragraph:
- Original: προσφέρω

- Transliteration: Prosphero

- Phonetic: pros-fer'-o

- Definition:

1. to bring to, lead to

a. one to a person who can heal him or is ready to show him some kindness, one to a person who is to judge him

b. to bring a present or a thing, to reach or hand a thing to one

c. to put to

2. to be borne towards one, to attack, assail

a. to behave one's self towards one, deal with one

- Origin: from G4314 and G5342 (including its alternate)

- TDNT entry: 10:05,1

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4314 and G5342 (including its alternate); to bear towards that is lead to tender (especially to God) treat: - bring (to unto) deal with do offer (unto up) present unto put to.
